talk_metadata.talk_title: Technical considerations and architecture of the Genie Data Model
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "the Genie data model is open source, cohort agnostic, and the really key point here is it implements a dual pathway architecture"
Speaker said: "we looked to FHIR and mCODE for interoperability components, and then also the analytical framework of the OMOP Common Data Model"

key_data_gaps: No real-world outcome data presented, only model specifications

crispro_opportunity.description: Develop AI/NLP pipelines to accurately extract clinical variables from unstructured notes, addressing the manual abstraction gap highlighted by the speaker.
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker answered: "We have a variety of built-in checks... we also have QA processes that will happen once those data are collected... we do have some AI tools like NLP to automate this portion as well"
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO's computational spine can provide validated NLP models and ontology mapping to improve extraction accuracy and reduce manual curation errors.

cited_competitors.name: FHIR
cited_competitors.context: referenced as a standard used to inform the Genie model

cited_competitors.name: mCODE
cited_competitors.context: referenced as a standard used to inform the Genie model

cited_competitors.name: OMOP Common Data Model
cited_competitors.context: referenced as a standard used to inform the Genie model

unresolved_questions: What specific NLP methods will be used to train models for accurate extraction of unstructured data?
How will extensions to the data model be versioned and shared back to the consortium?

talk_metadata.talk_title: Implementation of the Genie Data Model at Providence (GP3)
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "GP3 aims to replace or augment the manual data curation process so we can scale to larger cohorts and have better data quality"
Speaker said: "We use AI tools like GitHub Copilot to accelerate code writing and improve software engineering best practices"

key_data_gaps: Limited discussion of how multi-cancer patient records are reconciled across tables

crispro_opportunity.description: Apply CrisPRO's data lineage and validation framework to enhance GP3's QA/QC pipelines for heterogeneous source hierarchies.
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker said: "We implemented a lineage parser tool... it will automatically document the source of all of our attributes"
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO can provide automated provenance tracking and anomaly detection across mapped attributes, reducing manual lineage errors.

cited_competitors.name: GitHub Copilot
cited_competitors.context: mentioned as an AI code assistant used in the pipeline

cited_competitors.name: OMOP
cited_competitors.context: mentioned as part of Providence's data warehouse

unresolved_questions: How will the pipeline handle patients with multiple primary cancers when aggregating data across tables?
What strategies are planned to incorporate unstructured data extraction at scale?

talk_metadata.talk_title: European perspective on Genie, national cancer nodes, and clinical implementation
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "Genie has shown that multi-institutional data sharing is feasible, and with these layers of data sharing and the Candle project, the EU is now trying to scale this up to up to 20 nations"
Speaker said: "In the GenomeDE project, health insurance pays for sequencing only after a confirmation token is returned, creating a financial incentive for data submission"

key_data_gaps: No quantitative outcome data on survival benefit from the German MTB implementation
Limited evidence on data validation metrics across national nodes

crispro_opportunity.description: Leverage CrisPRO to provide standardized QA/QC metrics for the German cBioPortal MTB extension, addressing the speaker's concern about internal validation.
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker said: "I'm concerned about the validation and internal QA of the model... we do a lot of interlaboratory testing where we compare pipelines... it's still a big problem"
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO can generate reproducible validation dashboards and cross-site consistency reports, strengthening confidence in the MTB data pipeline.

crispro_opportunity.description: Use CrisPRO to identify under‑captured social determinants and comorbidity data that the speaker noted as a model weakness, enabling richer biomarker analyses.
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker said: "We built a more robust section for capturing social determinants of health"
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO's integrative analytics can combine SDOH variables with genomic data to discover population‑specific biomarkers.

cited_competitors.name: cBioPortal
cited_competitors.context: adapted for molecular tumor boards in Germany

cited_competitors.name: CANDLE consortium
cited_competitors.context: mentioned as a European effort to harmonize data

cited_competitors.name: GenomeDE
cited_competitors.context: described as a German reimbursement‑driven sequencing program using the Genie model

unresolved_questions: What quantitative QA/QC metrics are currently used to validate the cBioPortal MTB implementation?
How will the European Health Data Space integrate heterogeneous national cancer nodes technically?
